title: IronGate Enterprise Managed Policy
description: Managed policy schema for IronGate Enterprise. Set via Chrome Enterprise managed policy (Microsoft Intune, Jamf Pro, Google Workspace Admin, Active Directory Group Policy). End users cannot override these settings. The deployment mode is locked at extension startup.

deploymentMode.title: Deployment mode
deploymentMode.description: How IronGate handles AMBER-zone classification. 'local-only' = use local LLM, fail closed if unreachable. 'hybrid' = prefer local, fall back to cloud. 'server-only' = legacy cloud-only mode. Sovereign AI customers must use 'local-only'.

localEndpoint.title: Local LLM endpoint URL
localEndpoint.description: URL of the local LLM inference service. Must be a localhost URL (http://localhost:* or http://127.0.0.1:*) when deploymentMode is 'local-only'. Default for Ollama is http://localhost:11434/api/generate.

localModel.title: Local LLM model name
localModel.description: Name of the model to use. For Ollama deployments use the Ollama tag (e.g., 'llama3.2:3b'). IronGate has validated these models for production: llama3.2:3b (recommended), gemma4:e2b. Models known to underperform: gemma3:4b, phi3:mini, qwen2.5:3b, all 1B models.

localFormat.title: Local API format
localFormat.description: Wire format used by the local LLM endpoint. Use 'ollama' for Ollama. Use 'chrome-builtin' to route through Chrome's built-in Gemini Nano (zero-install path, requires Chrome 138+ with Prompt API enabled).

timeoutMs.title: Per-prompt timeout (ms)
timeoutMs.description: How long to wait for the local LLM to classify a single prompt before failing. Default 30 seconds — generous because cold inference can take 5-15s on first call.

auditLogDestination.title: Audit log destination
auditLogDestination.description: Where IronGate sends audit logs. Default 'none' = no logs leave the device (maximum privacy). 's3' / 'syslog' / 'webhook' = customer-controlled destination. 'irongate-dashboard' = optional opt-in to managed dashboard.

auditLogConfig.title: Audit log destination config
auditLogConfig.description: Destination-specific config (S3 bucket and credentials, syslog host, webhook URL, etc.). Format depends on auditLogDestination value.

policyBundleUrl.title: Signed policy bundle URL
policyBundleUrl.description: HTTPS URL where IronGate fetches signed detection policy bundles. Customer-controlled — points at the customer's own policy server, not IronGate's. Bundles are signed with the customer's Ed25519 key. Leave empty to use the bundled default policy.

pseudonymKey.title: Per-firm pseudonymization key
pseudonymKey.description: 32-byte (64 hex char) key used to deterministically generate pseudonyms. The same original entity always produces the same fake name across users in the same firm, enabling forensic correlation in audit logs while preserving privacy. Must be generated by IT and rotated periodically.

killSwitch.title: Organization-wide kill switch
killSwitch.description: If true, blocks ALL AI tool requests for users in this deployment. Used for emergency response (active incident, compliance hold, legal hold). When killSwitch is true, IronGate displays a notice explaining the block and the contact for the policy admin.

firmId.title: Firm identifier
firmId.description: Internal customer identifier for audit log correlation. Stored locally only — never transmitted to IronGate servers.

enrollmentCode.title: Enrollment Code
enrollmentCode.description: Organization enrollment code for automatic firm enrollment. Set by IT admin from the IronGate dashboard. When present, the extension auto-enrolls without user interaction.

supportContact.title: Internal IT support contact
supportContact.description: How users in this deployment should contact their internal IT for IronGate issues. Displayed in error messages and the sidepanel. Example: 'helpdesk@firm.com' or 'Slack #it-help'.

allowedAITools.title: Allowed AI tools
allowedAITools.description: List of AI tool platform IDs the user is allowed to use. Empty array = all supported tools allowed. Example: ['chatgpt', 'claude'] blocks Gemini and others. Tool IDs match the adapter id (chatgpt, claude, gemini, copilot, deepseek, perplexity, poe, groq, huggingface, you).
